symptom: Customer cannot delete Resource Group, VNET, or Subnet. Deletion fails because ACI Service Association Link (SAL) is blocking the operation on the subnet.
rootCause: ACI creates Service Association Links (SAL) on subnets used by container groups. When container groups are deleted but the SAL persists (dangling SAL), it blocks subnet/VNET/RG deletion.
solution: Follow the ACI SAL deletion TSG for CSS/customer mitigations first (see TSG: Cannot delete ACI Vnet or Subnet due to dangling Service Association Link). If all mitigations fail, escalate to EEE ACI via IcM for ACIS Action NRP/DeleteServiceAssociationLink. This action requires OaaS and R2D (SafeFly) clearance, onboarded by EEE ACI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/List of ACI issues where IcM is required

symptom: Customer cannot delete VNET or Subnet used by ACI. Orphan NICs are shown in the ACI Network Profile, blocking deletion even after container groups have been removed.
rootCause: When ACI container groups deployed in a VNET are deleted, their NICs may remain in the Network Profile as orphans, preventing subnet/VNET deletion.
solution: Validate that all Container Groups are deleted (check ASC). Confirm lingering NICs are shown in Network Profile in ASC. Escalate to EEE ACI via IcM for ACIS Action ACI/Remove Unused CNI. Only EEE ACI and PG have access to the required JIT clearance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/List of ACI issues where IcM is required

symptom: Customer cannot delete ACI Container Group. The deletion operation fails with an internal error.
rootCause: Backend issue (related to Emerging Issue 97623) prevents the container group from being properly stopped and deleted through normal API calls.
solution: Validate the issue via ASI detectors on both the CG page and caas page (look for failed stop deployment errors). Escalate to EEE ACI via IcM for ACIS Action ACI/Delete Container Group Skip Decryption to force-delete the CG from backend.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/List of ACI issues where IcM is required
